Purpose

Only graduated users are allowed to log in as alumni.

Data Source (UPDATED)

Display only users from the students table whose status is graduating.

Admin can:

Select individual users and approve them, or

Use a “Select All” → “Approve All” option.

When the Approve button is clicked:

Show a confirmation modal (glassmorphism design).

Example message:
“Are you sure you want to approve the selected student(s) as alumni?”

No action is taken.

If Yes / Confirm is clicked:

Update the selected student(s):

status → alumni

year → current year (or the configured approval year)

Send an email notification using EmailJS informing the student that they can now log in.

Use EmailJS to notify approved students.

Use the provided files only as a reference.

Do not reuse the entire page or UI code.

Add a condition:

If the user’s status is not alumni, display:

Admin can set a date that will automatically approve users.

When the date is reached:

Automatically approve all users whose status is graduating:

year → configured year

Send notification emails using EmailJS.

this are the new enum types for the current and first job levels. change the current enum types on ai services that has this functions:

Rank and File, Supervisory, Managerial, Intern, Trainee, Entry-Level, Senior Staff, Department Head, Director, Vice President, Executive

Intern

A temporary position typically held by students or fresh graduates, focused on gaining practical experience and industry exposure under close supervision.

Trainee

An entry position intended to develop job-specific skills through structured training programs, often leading to regular employment upon completion.

Entry-Level

A full-time position for newly hired employees with minimal professional experience, responsible for basic tasks and learning organizational processes.

Rank and File

Non-supervisory employees who perform operational, technical, or clerical duties and do not have authority over other employees.

Senior Staff

Experienced employees with advanced skills or expertise who may provide guidance or mentorship but do not hold formal managerial authority.

Supervisory

Employees responsible for overseeing the work of rank-and-file staff, ensuring tasks are completed correctly and according to organizational standards.

Managerial

Positions with authority to plan, organize, and manage teams or departments, including decision-making responsibilities and performance management.

Department Head

A senior leadership role responsible for the overall management, strategy, and performance of a specific department or functional area.

Director

2.  **Modals**: Increase modal z-indices to **9999** to ensure they float above everything else within that new layer.
